sourceMovie.title: Megan Is Missing
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Megan Is Missing works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Peeping Tom
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Megan Is Missing's drama stayed with you, Peeping Tom (1960) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The horror through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Reality
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Megan Is Missing excels in drama, Reality (2023) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Devils
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Devils (1971) is the natural next step after Megan Is Missing: same drama DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and horror elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Z
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Megan Is Missing who respond to its horror will find Z (2019) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Witchfinder General
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Megan Is Missing fans keep landing on Witchfinder General (1968) — the drama is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Goosebumps
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Goosebumps draws from the same horror well as Megan Is Missing, channeling adventure in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2015 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Beastly
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Beastly (2011) captures the drama appeal of Megan Is Missing without simply imitating it. The romance streak running through both films is what puts Beastly at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Holes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama appeal of Megan Is Missing — that funny edge, the adventure — translates cleanly into Holes (2003). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Deep End of the Ocean
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Deep End of the Ocean (1999) clearly understood what makes drama work — the same quality that keeps Megan Is Missing rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Cassandra Crossing
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Cassandra Crossing (1976) is the kind of film Megan Is Missing fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The thriller overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

faq.question: What should I watch after Megan Is Missing?
faq.answer: Try Peeping Tom, Reality, The Devils—each shares DNA with Megan Is Missing while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Megan Is Missing?
faq.answer: Megan Is Missing (2011) is best described as Drama, Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Megan Is Missing worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
